skill.name: standup-formatter
skill.description: Formats work activity into concise daily standup, team status, and project update summaries.
skill.summary: Create concise Done, Today, and Blockers updates for standups.
skill.author: ChaiWithJai

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the static external command findings because the flagged backticks are Markdown code fences in examples and templates, not executable code. The weak cryptographic algorithm finding at SKILL.md:3 is also a false positive; no cryptographic operation or prompt injection attempt was found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged backticks are Markdown fences around standup examples and templates. They do not execute shell, Ruby, or any external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are fenced Markdown examples and templates. No command invocation, interpolation, script block, or executable tool instruction appears in those locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged line is the skill description in frontmatter. It contains no hash algorithm, encryption call, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain descriptive metadata. There is no semantic evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any other weak cryptographic algorithm.

content.value_statement: Daily updates often become too long or vague for teams to act on. This skill turns work activity into concise Done, Today, and Blockers summaries for individual, team, and project standups.
actual_capabilities: Formats updates into Done, Today, and Blockers sections.
Supports individual, team, and project standup contexts.
Keeps written updates to three to five focused bullet points.
Adapts guidance for async written updates and sync spoken standups.
Prompts for timeframe and source type before formatting the update.
limitations: Does not collect Git activity or calendar events by itself.
Does not verify whether reported work items are accurate.
Does not manage team attendance, reminders, or posting schedules.
Does not replace detailed project reports or incident summaries.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Personal Daily Update
use_cases.description: Turn notes from yesterday and today into a short standup that highlights completed work, current focus, and blockers.

use_cases.title: Summarize Team Status
use_cases.description: Combine short inputs from several people into a consistent team standup format for Slack, email, or meeting notes.

use_cases.title: Report Project Progress
use_cases.description: Create a concise project update that separates outcomes, near-term work, and dependencies that need attention.

prompt_templates.title: Format My Daily Standup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner personal update from rough notes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Format this into a concise individual standup with Done, Today, and Blockers sections: [paste notes].

prompt_templates.title: Create an Async Team Update
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate team summary for written channels.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an async team standup from these updates. Keep each person concise and preserve links to tickets or PRs: [paste updates].

prompt_templates.title: Refine a Long Status Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced cleanup of a verbose update.
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this status draft as a standup update. Remove unnecessary detail, keep outcomes, and make blockers specific: [paste draft].

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Project Standup
prompt_templates.prompt: Format this project activity into a standup summary with completed outcomes, current focus, and dependencies that need action: [paste activity].

output_examples.input: Finished auth review, starting billing API work, waiting for backend contract.
output_examples.output: Done: Completed auth review. Today: Starting billing API integration. Blockers: Waiting for the backend API contract.

output_examples.input: Team notes from Alex and Priya about search fixes, release testing, and missing staging credentials.
output: Alex: Done: Fixed search filter issue. Today: Release testing. Blockers: None.
Priya: Done: Prepared staging checklist. Today: Validate deployment path. Blockers: Needs staging credentials.

output_examples.input: Weekly project notes with completed migration tasks, next feature work, and vendor approval delay.
output_examples.output: Done: Completed migration tasks. Today: Begin next feature work. Blockers: Vendor approval is delaying final rollout.

best_practices: Provide outcomes, not every activity performed.
Name blockers with the owner or decision needed.
Keep source notes short before asking for the final format.
anti_patterns: Do not include every meeting, message, or minor task.
Do not hide blockers behind vague phrases like waiting or stuck.
Do not use the format for detailed retrospectives or audit reports.

faq.question: Can this format both individual and team standups?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes guidance for individual updates, team summaries, and project status contexts.

faq.question: Does it read Git history automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can use Git activity as source material only when the user provides that information.

faq.question: What sections does it produce?
faq.answer: It focuses on Done, Today, and Blockers so the update stays concise and actionable.

faq.question: Is it useful for async standups?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports written async updates with slightly more detail and links when needed.

faq.question: Can it make updates shorter?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to remove low-value detail and keep three to five focused bullets.

faq.question: Does it create project reports?
faq.answer: It creates brief project standup summaries, not full project reports or formal status documents.
